free_text: Exploratory study of fission product yields of
Engineering Model FN Tandem Van de Graaff accelerator
at the Triangle Universities Nuclear Laboratory
Dual fission chambers, located at 2.54 cm
from the front side of gas cell. Activation foil was
located in the center of the chamber. The efficiency
of the dual-fission chambers of the present design
Gamma rays from fission products were measured
using HPGE detectors with 60 % efficiency, located
Fission yields were determined by activation
method using characteristic gamma rays of fission
An unpulsed 2 mu-A deuteron beam of energy 6.40
MeV enters through a 6.5 mu-m Havar foil the gas
cell. the resulting 6.0-MeV deuteron beam enters a
3.0-cm long cell filled with 6.9 atm of high-purity
deuterium gas. In traversing the gas cell the
deuterium beam loses < 0.53 MeV in the gas and is
then stopped in a 0.3-mm-thick tantalum disk at the
end of the gas cell. The resulting neutron beam from
the 2H(d,n) 3He reaction at 0 deg has a calculated
mean energy of 9.00 MeV with an energy spread of 0.54
MeV (FWHM). Neutrons of 4.6 MeV were produced via the
i) neutron flux differences on sample and reference
oil caused by close geometry (up to 10%)
ii)   efficiencies of dual-fission chamber caused
iii)  change in the effective fission cross section
due to the isotopic content of the target
relative to that of the reference foil (1%)
